rulings.Case Title: ALLYSYN OVERTON VS. PATRICK LAKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion For Reconsideration And Modification Of Order Re: Motion To Comply, Or In The Alternative To Unfile And Seal Records
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF ALLYSYN OVERTON's Motion For Reconsideration And Modification Of Order Re: Motion To Comply, Or In The Alternative To Unfile And Seal Records: To be heard by the Honorable Lillian Sing at 2:00 p.m. in department 501. (501/REQ) LATE JUDGE SING TENTATIVE: Denied as follows. Motion for reconsideration is denied as CCP 1008 is not applicable under the circumstances as moving party seeks the order of the court to be conformed to what the moving party believes was the Court's oral ruling. Motion to seal is denied without prejudice for Plaintiff to re-file it on proper notice and in compliance with CRC 2.550 and 2.551. Parties are ordered to advise the Court by 4:30 p.m. on October 9, 2014 whether the tentative ruling is contested and parties are going to appear for an oral argument. The notification shall be made by phone (415-551-3779) or e-mail (D501@sftc.org). (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: PARK LANE ASSOCIATES, LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. JOSEPH ALIOTO, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Amending Order And Judgment Dated June 9, 2014
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANTS JOSEPH ALIOTO and MICHELE ALIOTO's Motion For An Order Amending Order And Judgment Dated June 9, 2014: DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden under CCP 473(b). (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALFONSO PORTILLO, TRUSTEE OF THE ALFONSO PORTILLO VS. TINA SUY TROUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Plaintiff'S Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANT CLAUDIA ROJAS' Motion To Strike Portions Of The Plaintiff'S Third Amended Complaint: GRANTED with leave to amend as follows: (1) Plaintiff should either remove the allegation "Plaintiff demands possession from each defendant because of expiration of a fixed term lease" from the complaint, or in good faith allege a separate/alternative cause of action for unlawful detainer based on expiration of fixed term lease; (2) Plaintiff shall properly verify the complaint and state that its contents are "true of his own knowledge, except as to the matters which are therein stated on his or her information or belief, and as to those matters that he or she believes it to be true." CCP 446(a). Defendant's motion to strike is an appropriate response to a new complaint. Causing v. San Francisco Unified School District (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 1224. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHIU CHAN VS. WENHAO FAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANT XIUCHAN ZHAO's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND: Hearing Required. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TIPTON MANAGEMENT COMPANY VS. MICHAEL SISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANT MICHAEL SISCO's Motion To set aside/Vacate Default and default judgment: Hearing Required. Defendant shall produce unredacted version of Exhibit A to his declaration for the Court's review. Defendant should be prepared to post $2,900 ($1,600 due per Notice, plus September and October rent) with the Court if motion is granted. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: QUINCY YU ET AL VS. JEFREY YU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT JEFFREY YU's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: Sustained with leave to amend to allege that rent became due within one year of service of notice. (CCP 1161(2)). Demurer is otherwise overruled. Complaint and Notice sufficiently allege rent default pursuant to an oral agreement under which the property is held; that Plaintiffs are the owners of the property and successors in interest to Defendants original landlord; that defendant was required to pay monthly rent of $1,800 to the landlord, etc. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTELOPE HALAWAN PROPERTIES, LLC VS. VICTOR GOMEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANTS VICTOR GOMEZ, EUGENIO GOMEZ and JOSE CAMOLI's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: Overruled, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges service of notice by post and mail on September 5, 2014 and notice expiration on September 5, 2014. Moving party cites no authority for the proposition that after hour mailing pushes the service date to the next day. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTELOPE HALAWAN PROPERTIES, LLC VS. HUGO NELSON CHOX GUARCHAJ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 8: DEFENDANTS LIEDA VEGA, VILMA VEGA, HUGO NELSON and HUGO PEREZ's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: Overruled, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges service of notice by post and mail on September 5, 2014 and notice expiration on September 5, 2014. Moving party cites no authority for the proposition that after hour mailing pushes the service date to the next day. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PRISCILLA HSUE VS. HOWARD RICHMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of A 5-Day Summons And Complaint For Unlawful Detainer
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 10, 2014, Line 9: DEFENDANTS HOWARD RICHMAN and CLAUDINE TRITTIN-RICHMAN's Motion To Quash Service Of A 5-Day Summons And Complaint For Unlawful Detainer: Hearing Required. (501/REQ)
